text: Wake up, Neo...
2:13 AM. Your apartment smells like cold coffee and dead circuits.
By day, you are Thomas A. Anderson. Programmer. Metacortex, floor 7, cubicle 9.
By night, you are something else. Something they don't have a file on. Yet.
Your terminal blinks. Then a message appears that you didn't write.

choices.label: Minimize it. You have work to finish.

text: You minimize the window.
You keep typing.
Three seconds pass.
The window maximizes itself.
The cursor blinks, waiting.
> THE MATRIX HAS YOU, NEO.
> KNOCK KNOCK.
Your hands have stopped moving. The room feels smaller than it did a moment ago.

choices.label: This is someone's idea of a joke. Close it.

text: You close it.
You sit in the dark for a while.
You go to bed at 3:12 AM.
At 3:47 AM, there is a knock at the door.
You were not asleep.
It's Choi. He owes you money. Tonight he's picking up a disk.
"Hallelujah. You're my savior, man. My own personal Jesus Christ."
He has people with him. Music bleeding from someone's headphones.
Then you see her. A girl near the back.
On her shoulder: a tattoo.
A white rabbit.
You close the door.
You go back to your terminal.
That is wrong. It was closer to 4 AM a moment ago.
Your terminal blinks. A message appears that you didn't write.
The bass is too loud. The lights are wrong.
You find a corner. You watch people move.
A woman appears beside you. She does not ask if the seat is taken.
"I know why you're here, Neo. I know what you've been afraid to ask."
Her name is Trinity. She knows yours.
His name is Morpheus.
He has two pills. One in each hand.
"You take the blue pill — the story ends. You wake up in your bed and believe whatever you want to believe."
"You take the red pill — you stay in Wonderland. And I show you how deep the rabbit hole goes."
He watches you the way people watch someone who is about to make a mistake they cannot undo.
You wake up.
Your bed. Your apartment. The alarm hasn't gone off yet.
It was a dream.
But your hand won't stop shaking.
The terminal blinks. A message appears.
Same words.
The clock says 2:13 AM.

choices.label: Ignore it. Go back to sleep.

text: 2:13 AM again.
You have been here before. You know you have.
The coffee is cold in the same way. The cursor blinks at the same interval.
Something is wrong with time.
Morpheus offered you something. You said no.
You keep saying no.
But there is another way out.

choices.label: I remember. Take me back to the choice.

text: You have been here for a very long time now.
You do not remember what came before this.
Morpheus. Trinity. The choice.
They feel like something you dreamed about once.
The exit is not here anymore.
Cold.
The first thing you feel is cold.
Then: metal. Tubes. The sound of something releasing.
You open your eyes in a body you have never used before.
"Welcome to the real world."
The lights are harsh. Everything is rust and wire and silence.
This is what is left of the world.
White. Infinite white.
"This is the construct. A loading program."
Morpheus explains everything. The machines. The harvest. The dream you were living.
Your muscles have atrophied. Your eyes have never focused in real light before.
But here, in the white, anything can exist.
He asks you: what do you believe is possible?

choices.label: "I believe what I can measure. What I can prove."
"I don't know what I believe yet. But I'm willing."

text: Her kitchen smells like cookies.
She looks at you the way people look at something they already know the answer to.
"I'm going to let you in on a little secret. Being the One is just like being in love. No one can tell you you are. You just know it."
Then she pauses.
She looks past Neo.
She looks at you.
Not at the character. Not at Thomas Anderson.
At you.
"You've been here before," she says quietly. "Haven't you."
The call comes in the middle of a training run.
Cypher. It was Cypher.
He made a deal — his re-insertion into the Matrix in exchange for Morpheus.
Morpheus is in the hands of the Agents now.
They will break him. Extract the codes.
Tank is looking at you. Waiting.

choices.label: "We're going in. Load the jump program."

text: The lobby.
Marble floors. Security station. Cameras.
You move like something trained for exactly this.
Because you have been.
Floors blur. You find him.
Now there is only one thing standing between Morpheus and the exit.
He is waiting on the roof.
He moves faster than physics should allow.
"Mr. Anderson. Do you hear that? That is the sound of inevitability."
He catches your strike before it lands.
"It is the sound of your death."
The city is far below. The wind is very cold.
You look at his hand holding your fist.
And you think about spoons.

choices.label: Fight your way to the exit.

text: The phone is ringing.
It is across the room.
An Agent is 40 feet behind you and closing.
You can hear the footsteps.
30 feet.
The phone is still ringing.
20 feet.

choices.label: You freeze. You cannot make it.

text: The city cracks open.
You see it — for the first time, you actually see it.
Not the buildings. Not the people.
The code.
Cascading green. Everything made of it. Everyone.
You understand now what Morpheus meant.
What the Oracle knew.
You are not the One because someone told you.
You are the One because you stopped waiting to be told.
SYSTEM SECURE.
The Agents are very efficient.
It does not hurt. That is almost the worst part.
Somewhere in a server farm the size of a country, a process completes.
Thomas A. Anderson is reintegrated.
Mr. Anderson returns to his desk on floor 7, cubicle 9.
The Matrix continues.
It always continues.
It has been 2:13 AM for longer than you can calculate.
You know what you were supposed to do.
You always knew.
But knowing and believing are different countries.
You could not find the border crossing in time.
Morpheus offered you a door.
You kept choosing the wall.
The door does not come twice.
